title: Deliver versioned MCP initialization instructions
goal: Bind a small versioned and hash-verifiable operator instruction contract to FastMCP initialization so every compatible client session receives the truth hierarchy, narrow-read-first rule, mutation preconditions, fail-closed retry rule and typed-operation preference without requiring an active read of GRABOWSKI.md.

acceptance.assertion: The instruction text is generated from or checked against one versioned canonical source with an explicit schema version, SHA-256 and bounded byte size.
FastMCP is instantiated with the exact canonical instructions and the running deployment proves the same instruction identity.

The instructions contain only the truth hierarchy, narrow typed read first, mutation target and validation requirements, unchanged-retry prohibition after ambiguous failures, and typed-operation preference.
Instructions do not grant action, merge, deploy, secret or retry authority and explicitly preserve live receipts and policy as higher-order truth.
Tests fail on missing instructions, stale generated identity, oversize content, semantic key loss or divergence between source, runtime entrypoint and deployment manifest.
After merge and commit-bound deployment, a live initialization or equivalent server-contract readback proves the instruction hash and version without claiming universal client compliance.

evidence_basis: current client uses deferred tool discovery
metadata.block_reason: The implementation and direct MCP initialize handshake are prepared, but the only remaining generated file, docs/generated/operator-context.v1.json, is held by an active foreign T037 lease. The lease is on the exact shared artifact and cannot be bypassed as a non-conflicting exception.

implementation.head: c9caabab33ccd61f4eca20b318f058efb0d0f505
implementation.full_tests: 1363 passed, 1 blocked by stale generated operator context

metadata.next_action: After T037 integrates and releases the exact generated-context lease, rebase PR #175, acquire that file, run context-refresh, make validate and make deploy-check, then bind the final GitHub diff and critical self-review before merge.
